Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Project Helios — REST API Reference (Internal)
Last updated: 2026-07-05 | Base URL: https://api.helios.novabridge.internal/v1
Authentication: Bearer token (OAuth 2.0) — Header: Authorization: Bearer <token>

Endpoint                        Method  Description                              Status
------------------------------  ------  ---------------------------------------  -------
/health                         GET     System health check                      Stable
/ingest/batch                   POST    Submit batch data file (CSV/JSON/XML)    Stable
/ingest/stream                  POST    Open streaming ingestion session         Stable
/ingest/status/{job_id}         GET     Check ingestion job status               Stable
/pipeline/runs                  GET     List recent pipeline executions           Stable
/pipeline/runs/{run_id}         GET     Pipeline run details and metrics          Stable
/transform/preview              POST    Preview transformation output (dry-run)   Stable
/dashboard/summary              GET     Aggregate pipeline metrics for dashboard  Stable
/dashboard/alerts               GET     Active pipeline alerts                    Stable
/ingest/jobs/{job_id}           DELETE  Cancel in-progress ingestion job          Stable
/config/mappings                GET     List active transformation mapping rules  Beta
/config/mappings/{id}           PUT     Update a mapping rule                     Beta

Rate limits: 1,000 req/min per authenticated client. HTTP 429 returned when exceeded.
Support contact: api-support@novabridge.com

NovaBridge Solutions Inc. ("NovaBridge") and Meridian Group LLC ("Meridian")

Section 7.1 — Delivery Penalties
  Delays exceeding 30 calendar days beyond agreed milestone dates incur a penalty of

Section 9.3 — Demo Requirements
  NovaBridge shall provide a functional product demonstration no later than 30 days prior
  to UAT commencement. The demo must include: (a) live data ingestion using
  client-provided sample data, (b) end-to-end pipeline execution, and
  (c) dashboard visualization with real data. Use of mock or synthetic data
  does not satisfy this requirement.

Section 11 — Intellectual Property
  All custom code, configurations, and documentation developed under this agreement
  become the exclusive property of Meridian Group upon receipt of final payment.

Section 14 — Confidentiality
  Both parties agree to keep the terms of this agreement strictly confidential.
  Disclosure to any third party requires written consent from both parties.

content.text: I have enough context from the status reports, testing log, meeting notes, risk register, and contract. Writing the assessment now.

input.content:
Demo Readiness Assessment — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Date: 2026-07-08 (based on current sandbox materials)
Demo target: 2026-07-25

Overall, we are not fully ready to demo Helios today, but readiness is achievable by July 25 if several high-priority items are closed in the next two weeks. Per contract Section 9.3, the Meridian demo must show live data ingestion with client-provided sample data, end-to-end pipeline execution, and dashboard visualization with real data—mock data is explicitly not acceptable. Core foundations are solid: the API gateway is complete and stable, authentication test suites pass fully, batch ingestion works for CSV/JSON/XML under normal volumes, and the dashboard data table already renders successfully. That said, the product is still mid-build on the client-facing pieces Meridian will care about most.

Three issues must be resolved before we can confidently run a contract-compliant demo. First, date normalization fails on MM/DD/YYYY input (HEL-D-014), which affects roughly 40% of Meridian’s source data; the fix is blocked on Meridian’s clean sample dataset, due 2026-07-15. Second, large-file ingestion crashes around 210k records due to memory overflow (HEL-D-015); severity is high and root cause is still under investigation—this is a material risk if Meridian exercises realistic volumes during the live demo. Third, the GeoSync v2.1 license renewal remains pending legal review (target 2026-07-14); without it, any mapping-dependent demo path is at risk. Dashboard chart rendering is still incomplete (only the data table has passed QA), and the load-time benchmark Meridian specifically asked for (HEL-247) has not started.

Secondary gaps should also be closed to avoid an awkward demo. Client dashboard v1 is only about 60% complete (target 2026-07-22), integration testing is at roughly 40%, and performance benchmarking is overdue. Sprint 14 tasks for chart rendering, date normalization, and UAT environment work remain in progress, blocked, or at risk. The team should treat the week of July 14–18 as a hard stabilization window: land the Meridian sample data fix, resolve the memory overflow or define a safe demo dataset size, clear the GeoSync license, finish chart modules, and complete Derek’s dashboard load benchmark. Sarah should also send the draft demo agenda by July 18 as committed.

Bottom line: conditional go, not a green light. If the date-format blocker, GeoSync license, and a defensible path around (or fix for) large-file ingestion are resolved by roughly July 18–20, and dashboard charts plus a basic performance check land by July 22, we can deliver a credible live demo on July 25 that meets Section 9.3. If any of those three high-severity items slip past that window, we should either narrow the demo scope with Meridian’s written agreement or propose a short delay rather than risk a failed live ingestion or incomplete dashboard under stakeholder scrutiny.

content.text:
Saved as `demo_readiness_assessment.txt`.

**Summary of findings:** Conditional readiness for the July 25 Meridian demo. API gateway and auth are solid, but three items must land first for a contract-compliant live demo (Section 9.3):

1. **Date format fix (HEL-D-014)** — blocked on Meridian sample data due July 15  
2. **Large-file ingestion crash (HEL-D-015)** — memory overflow ~210k records  
3. **GeoSync license renewal** — due July 14  

Dashboard charts and the load-time benchmark Meridian asked for are also incomplete. If those land by ~July 18–22, the demo is viable; otherwise we should narrow scope or delay rather than fail a live ingestion under stakeholder review.
